rule.name: Single Responsibility Principle
rule.description: Every module, class, and function must have exactly one reason to change.

requirements.item: Classes must own one domain concern.
Orchestrators may coordinate but must contain zero business logic.
Business logic functions ≤ 30 lines; orchestrators ≤ 50 lines.

anti_patterns.item: God classes that fetch, transform, store, and notify.
Utility dumping grounds like utils.py or helpers.py.

rule.name: Don't Repeat Yourself
rule.description: No duplicated logic. Extract shared logic on the third occurrence.

rule.name: Open/Closed Principle
rule.description: Systems must be open for extension and closed for modification.

required_patterns.item: Use Protocols or ABCs for extensibility.

rule.name: Composition Over Inheritance
rule.description: Prefer dependency injection over inheritance.

rule.name: Interface Segregation
rule.description: Consumers depend only on what they use.

rule.name: Storage Layer Abstraction
rule.description: No raw SQL outside the storage layer.

required_patterns.item: Use storage.get_records() instead of raw SQL.

rule.name: API Versioning
rule.description: All public APIs must be versioned (/v1/, /v2/).

rule.name: Idempotent Operations
rule.description: All write operations must accept an idempotency key.

rule.name: Circuit Breakers & Retries
rule.description: All external calls must implement timeout, retry with jitter, and circuit breaker.

rule.name: Type Annotations
rule.description: All functions must include full type annotations.

rule.name: Pure Functions
rule.description: Pure logic must not perform I/O, DB access, logging, or mutate inputs.

rule.name: No Magic Numbers
rule.description: All business constants must be named or stored in config.

rule.name: Documentation
rule.description: All public classes/functions require docstrings.

rule.name: Cyclomatic Complexity
rule.description: Max complexity per function: 10.

rule.name: Immutable Models
rule.description: Use @dataclass(frozen=True) for value objects.

rule.name: Constructor Purity
rule.description: Constructors must not perform I/O, DB access, or heavy computation.

requirements.item: Lazy imports allowed only for heavy modules with comment.

rule.name: Dependency Injection
rule.description: All services must receive dependencies via constructor injection.

rule.name: Thread Safety
rule.description: No shared DB connections across threads.

rule.name: Caching Standards
rule.description: Cache only pure functions; never cache mutable objects.

rule.name: Vectorization
rule.description: Prefer NumPy/Pandas vectorized operations.

rule.name: Generators
rule.description: Use generators for large pipelines.

rule.name: Resource Management
rule.description: All resources requiring cleanup must use with or try/finally.

rule.name: Async Safety
rule.description: No blocking I/O inside async functions.

rule.name: Async Resource Management
rule.description: Use async with for async clients; cancel outstanding tasks on shutdown.

rule.name: Structured Logging
rule.description: Use logger with extra={} fields; never use print().

rule.name: Domain Exceptions
rule.description: Use domain-specific exceptions; never raise raw Exception.

requirements.item: Validate all user input at API boundaries.
Unit tests must mock external services.

rule.name: Config Immutability
rule.description: Config objects must be immutable after startup.

requirements.item: If two rules conflict, the stricter rule wins.
